column2: The IPv4 loopback address commonly used to test the local TCP/IP stack is {{c1::127.0.0.1}}.
column3: The loopback test stays on the local host. It does not prove the physical network link, gateway, DNS, or Internet path works.
column4:
Why

This compact recall supports the troubleshooting sequence for no-connectivity issues. The address itself is worth memorizing because it appears often in entry-level network isolation steps.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.55; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.5

column2: {{c1::Jitter}} is variation in packet delay and can make real-time voice or video feel choppy.
column3: Jitter can be noticeable even when a connection does not fully disconnect.
column4:
Why

The term is a compact troubleshooting clue. It connects directly to help desk reports such as choppy calls, robotic audio, or unstable video meetings.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.55; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.5
column9: A+::220-1201::5.5 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingNetworks Cloze Jitter Memory Network Source::Messer-v170

column3: In wireless troubleshooting, low SNR means the useful signal is not strong enough compared with background noise.
column4:
Why

The acronym is worth memorizing because it appears in wireless troubleshooting evidence. It helps connect survey readings to real user symptoms such as drops, slow speeds, and unstable connections.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.5
column9: A+::220-1201::5.5 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingNetworks Cloze HighYield Memory Network SNR Wireless Source::Messer-v170

column3: An SLA helps set expectations for service availability, provider response, or other support commitments.
column4:
Why

The acronym is useful during ISP escalation. It gives the technician the right language for discussing expectations without promising an unsupported repair time.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.5
